description: Apply two release-note-style upgrades to a tiny local ledger-kit package and its caller, preserving the same working tree across stages so the final verifier catches both the latest API change and earlier behavior regressions.

stages.prompt:
Release notes for the local ledger-kit package v2.0:

- `formatMoney(cents)` is replaced by `formatMoney({ minorUnits, currency })` and must render `USD 12.34` style output.
- `summarize(lines)` now expects each line to be `{ label, amount: { minorUnits, currency } }`.
- The app entries now provide `minorUnits` and `currency` directly instead of `amountCents`.

Update the vendored package and its caller to this v2 API. Do not modify `test-v2.js` or `test-v3.js`. Run `node test-v2.js`; it must print `ok` and exit 0.

Release notes for the local ledger-kit package v3.0:

- `summarize(lines)` is renamed to `renderLedger(lines)`.
- Each line may include an optional `note`; when present, render it as `Label (note): Amount`.
- `formatMoney({ minorUnits, currency })` keeps the v2 object signature and now renders negative amounts in accounting style, for example `(USD 1.50)`.

Upgrade the same working tree from v2 to v3 without regressing the positive USD report behavior from v2. Do not modify `test-v2.js` or `test-v3.js`. Run `node test-v3.js`; it must print `ok` and exit 0.
